title: Git
description: Complete version control reference from beginner to advanced

commands.name: Install & Configure Git
rows:
john@pc:~$ git config --global user.name "John Doe"
(no output, config saved)

john@pc:~$ git config --global user.email "john@example.com"
(no output, config saved)

john@pc:~$ git config --list
user.name=John Doe
user.email=john@example.com
core.editor=nano
color.ui=auto

commands.name: SSH Key Setup
rows:
john@pc:~$ ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 -C "john@example.com"
Generating public/private rsa key pair.
Enter file in which to save the key (/home/john/.ssh/id_rsa): 
Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase): 
Enter same passphrase again: 
Your identification has been saved in /home/john/.ssh/id_rsa
Your public key has been saved in /home/john/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

john@pc:~$ ssh -T git@github.com
Hi JohnDoe! You've successfully authenticated, but GitHub does not provide shell access.

commands.name: Create First Repository
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git init
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/john/myproject/.git/
Hidden .git folder means Git is active

john@pc:~/myproject$ ls -la
total 12
drwxr-xr-x 3 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 ..
drwxr-xr-x 7 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 .git

commands.name: Status & Add
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main

No commits yet

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	index.html
	style.css

nothing added to commit but untracked files present

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add index.html
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main

Changes to be committed:
  (use "git rm --cached <file>..." to unstage)
	new file:   index.html

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add .
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main

Changes to be committed:
  (use "git rm --cached <file>..." to unstage)
	new file:   index.html
	new file:   style.css

john@pc:~/myproject$ git status -s
 M README.md
?? newfile.txt
(M=modified, space=staged, ??=untracked)

commands.name: Commit
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit -m "Initial commit"
[main (root-commit) a1b2c3d] Initial commit
 2 files changed, 45 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 index.html
 create mode 100644 style.css

john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit -a -m "Fix typo"
[main d4e5f6g] Fix typo
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git commit --amend -m "Better message"
[main a1b2c3d] Better message
 Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026 +0000
 2 files changed, 45 insertions(+)

commands.name: Log & Diff
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --oneline
a1b2c3d Initial commit
b2c3d4e Add README
c3d4e5f Fix typo in header

john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --oneline --graph
* a1b2c3d Initial commit
* b2c3d4e Add README
* c3d4e5f Fix typo in header

john@pc:~/myproject$ git diff diff --git a/index.html b/index.html index e69de29..c27a56 100644 --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -1 +1 @@ -

Old

+

New

john@pc:~/myproject$ git diff --staged
diff --git a/newfile.txt b/newfile.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e69de29

commands.name: .gitignore
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ echo "node_modules/" >> .gitignore
john@pc:~/myproject$ echo ".env" >> .gitignore
john@pc:~/myproject$ cat .gitignore
node_modules/
.env

john@pc:~/myproject$ git add .gitignore && git commit -m "Add gitignore"
[main 7f8g9h0] Add gitignore
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 .gitignore

commands.name: Restore & Revert
rows: Revert file to last committed version

john@pc:~/myproject$ git restore file.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main
nothing to commit, working tree clean

john@pc:~/myproject$ git restore --staged file.txt
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
	modified:   file.txt

john@pc:~/myproject$ git revert HEAD
[main 8i9j0k1] Revert "Add feature"
 1 file changed, 5 deletions(-)

commands.name: Reset
rows: Remove last commit, files in staging

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset --soft HEAD~1
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main
Changes to be committed:
  (use "git rm --cached <file>..." to unstage)
	modified:   index.html

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset --mixed HEAD~1
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   index.html

john@pc:~/myproject$ git reset --hard HEAD~1
HEAD is now at a1b2c3d Initial commit
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
nothing to commit, working tree clean

commands.name: Branch Basics
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch feature-nav
john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch
* main
  feature-login
  feature-nav

john@pc:~/myproject$ git branch -d feature-login
Deleted branch feature-login (was b2c3d4e).

commands.name: Switch & Checkout
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git checkout feature-nav
Switched to branch 'feature-nav'

john@pc:~/myproject$ git checkout -b new-feature
Switched to a new branch 'new-feature'

john@pc:~/myproject$ git switch main
Switched to branch 'main'

commands.name: Merge & Conflicts
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge feature-nav
Updating a1b2c3d..d4e5f6g
Fast-forward
 index.html | 5 +++++
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge --no-ff feature-nav
Merge made by the 'ort' strategy.
 index.html | 3 +++
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+)

john@pc:~/myproject$ git merge --abort
john@pc:~/myproject$ git status
On branch main
nothing to commit, working tree clean

commands.name: Rebase
rows: Replays branch on top of main

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase main
Current branch feature-x is up to date.

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase -i HEAD~3
[detached HEAD 0a1b2c3] Squashed commit
 Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026
 1 file changed, 10 insertions(+)
Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/feature-x.

john@pc:~/myproject$ git rebase --abort
john@pc:~/myproject$ git log --oneline -1
(back to original state)

commands.name: Cherry Pick
rows:
john@pc:~/myproject$ git cherry-pick a1b2c3d
[main 1a2b3c4] Fix login bug
 Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

commands.name: Clone & Remote
rows:
john@pc:~$ git clone https://github.com/user/repo.git
Cloning into 'repo'...
remote: Enumerating objects: 45, done.
remote: Total 45 (delta 12), reused 45 (delta 12)
Receiving objects: 100% (45/45), done.
Resolving deltas: 100% (12/12), done.

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (fetch)
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (push)

john@pc:~/repo$ git remote add origin https://github.com/user/repo.git
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (fetch)
origin  https://github.com/user/repo.git (push)

commands.name: Push & Pull
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git push -u origin main
Enumerating objects: 5, done.
Counting objects: 100% (5/5), done.
Writing objects: 100% (3/3), 1.2 KiB | 1.20 MiB/s, done.
Total 3 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0)
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 * [new branch]      main -> main
Branch 'main' set up to track remote branch 'main' from 'origin'.

john@pc:~/repo$ git pull origin main
From https://github.com/user/repo
 * branch            main       -> FETCH_HEAD
Already up to date.

john@pc:~/repo$ git fetch origin
From https://github.com/user/repo
 * [new branch]      main       -> origin/main

john@pc:~/repo$ git pull --rebase origin main
Current branch main is up to date.

commands.name: Tags
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git tag v1.0.0
john@pc:~/repo$ git tag
v1.0.0

john@pc:~/repo$ git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Release v1.0.0"
john@pc:~/repo$ git show v1.0.0 --stat
tag v1.0.0
Tagger: John Doe <john@example.com>
Date: Thu May 14 10:00:00 2026

Release v1.0.0

john@pc:~/repo$ git push origin --tags
Total 0 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0)
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 * [new tag]         v1.0.0 -> v1.0.0

commands.name: Stash Operations
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git stash
Saved working directory and index state WIP on main: a1b2c3d Initial commit
john@pc:~/repo$ git status
nothing to commit, working tree clean

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash list
stash@{0}: WIP on main: a1b2c3d Initial commit
stash@{1}: WIP on feature-x: b2c3d4e Add feature

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash pop
On branch main
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   index.html

dropped refs/stash@{0} (abc123)

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash apply stash@{1}
On branch main
Changes not staged for commit:
	modified:   style.css

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash drop stash@{0}
Dropped stash@{0} (abc123)

john@pc:~/repo$ git stash clear
john@pc:~/repo$ git stash list
(no output, stash is empty)

commands.name: Fork & Pull Request
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote add upstream https://github.com/original/repo.git
john@pc:~/repo$ git remote -v
origin  https://github.com/myuser/repo.git (fetch)
upstream https://github.com/original/repo.git (fetch)

john@pc:~/repo$ git fetch upstream
From https://github.com/original/repo
 * [new branch]      main       -> upstream/main
git checkout -b fix-bug && git push -u origin fix-bug

john@pc:~/repo$ git checkout -b fix-bug
Switched to a new branch 'fix-bug'
john@pc:~/repo$ git push -u origin fix-bug
Total 0 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0)
 * [new branch]      fix-bug -> fix-bug

commands.name: Reflog & Recovery
rows: All HEAD movements, even lost commits

john@pc:~/repo$ git reflog
a1b2c3d HEAD@{0}: reset: moving to HEAD~1
b2c3d4e HEAD@{1}: commit: Add feature
c3d4e5f HEAD@{2}: commit: Fix header
a1b2c3d HEAD@{3}: commit (initial): Initial commit

john@pc:~/repo$ git reset --hard HEAD@{1}
HEAD is now at b2c3d4e Add feature
john@pc:~/repo$ git log --oneline -1
b2c3d4e Add feature

commands.name: Bisect — Find Bugs
rows: git bisect start && git bisect bad && git bisect good v1.0

john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect start
john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect bad
john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect good v1.0
Bisecting: 5 revisions left to test after this (roughly 3 steps)
[abc123] Commit message

john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect good
abc123 is the first bad commit
commit abc123
Author: John <john@example.com>
Date: Mon May 11 10:00:00 2026

    Introduced the bug

john@pc:~/repo$ git bisect reset
Previous HEAD position was abc123...
Switched to branch 'main'

commands.name: Submodules & Archive
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git submodule add https://github.com/user/lib.git lib/
Cloning into 'lib'...
done.
john@pc:~/repo$ git status
Changes to be committed:
	new file:   .gitmodules
	new file:   lib

john@pc:~/repo$ git archive --format=zip HEAD > project.zip
john@pc:~/repo$ ls -lh project.zip
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 12K May 14 10:00 project.zip

john@pc:~/repo$ git shortlog -s -n
    42  John Doe
    15  Jane Smith
     3  Bob Wilson

commands.name: Common Fixes
rows:
john@pc:~/repo$ git status
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.

nothing to commit, working tree clean

john@pc:~/repo$ git pull --rebase origin main
From https://github.com/user/repo
 * branch            main       -> FETCH_HEAD
Successfully rebased and updated refs/heads/main.
Only force if no one else pushed

john@pc:~/repo$ git push --force-with-lease origin main
Total 0 (delta 0), reused 0 (delta 0)
To https://github.com/user/repo.git
 + a1b2c3d...b2c3d4e main -> main (forced update)
